title: 10 best herbal creams for gum infection

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree creams helps with gum infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which effectively target and eliminate the bacteria responsible for causing gum inflammation. The cream's active ingredient, tea tree oil, has natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compounds that penetrate deep into the affected area, soothing swollen gums and reducing bleeding. As a result, it promotes healthy gum tissue regeneration and helps to alleviate symptoms such as pain, discomfort, and bad breath associated with gum infections.
remedy_properties: Tea Tree Oil's Antimicrobial Properties: Help combat bacterial and fungal infections by killing or inhibiting the growth of microorganisms, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Calming and Anti-Inflammatory Compounds: Soothe and reduce swelling in inflamed gums, making it an effective treatment for gum infections such as gingivitis and periodontitis.
Antioxidant Properties: Neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissue, preventing further inflammation and promoting the repair of damaged gum tissue.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in oils with antimicrobial properties, which help combat bacteria and reduce inflammation associated with gum infections.
Roots: Containing compounds that have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, effectively soothing swollen gums and preventing further infection.
remedy_recipe: Gather 200g of organic coconut oil and 30ml of distilled water.
Sterilize the equipment by autoclaving at 121°c for 15 minutes.
Infuse 20g of dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves in hot distilled water for 10 minutes.
Mix 50ml of the infused herbal liquid with 150g of melted coconut oil and 30ml of beeswax.
Heat the mixture under low heat until it reaches a temperature of 65°c and allows to cool.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with gum infection because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active compound, calendula, has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections, such as Streptococcus mutans and Porphyromonas gingivalis. Additionally, pot marigold's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation, promoting a healthy healing process and reducing the risk of chronic gum disease. This natural remedy provides a gentle and effective solution for soothing and treating gum infections.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Calendula, the active compound in pot marigold cream, exhibits antimicrobial properties that help to combat bacteria and fungi associated with gum infections, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Pot marigold cream's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with gum infections, creating a conducive environment for healthy tissue growth and repair.

Wound-healing and soothing properties: Calendula has been shown to stimulate wound healing by promoting collagen synthesis, improving tissue hydration, and reducing oxidative stress, making it an effective agent in treating gum infection-related wounds or lesions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and promoting healing in gum infections.
Seeds: Containing oil with antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial growth and reduce the risk of further infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers weighing about 100 grams and wash them thoroughly under cold running water.
Combine the cleaned calendula flowers with 500 milliliters of boiling distilled water in a stainless steel pot.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes before straining it through a cheesecloth into a glass bowl and discarding solids.
Mix 200 grams of virgin coconut oil with the resulting infused liquid and stir until fully incorporated at room temperature.
Pour the mixture into sterilized containers and store them in the refrigerator for up to six months or freeze for longer term.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with gum infection because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and ease pain, allowing for faster healing. The antimicrobial properties of ginger also help combat bacteria that cause infections, promoting a healthy balance in the mouth. Additionally, ginger's natural antioxidant properties help protect gums from further damage, accelerating the recovery process.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginger's antioxidant compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to neutralize free radicals and reduce inflammation in the gums, reducing the severity of gum infections.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in ginger, including gingerol and paradol, can help to reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections, promoting healing and preventing further damage.

Antimicrobial properties: Ginger's antimicrobial agents, such as gingerol and zingiberene, have been shown to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria that cause gum infections, including Streptococcus mutans and E. coli.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: These underground stems contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help reduce swelling and fight off bacterial infections in gums.
Roots: Ginger roots are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help soothe and calm irritated gums, reducing inflammation and pain.
remedy_recipe: Grind 2 tablespoons of dried ginger root into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 30 seconds.
Mix the ground ginger powder with 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a heat-resistant bowl until well combined for 5 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax pellets to the mixture and stir until fully incorporated and melted at 150°f for 10 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool slightly before whipping it with an electric mixer for 2 minutes.
Pour the cream into sterilized jars, seal tightly, and store in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with gum infection because of its potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compound curcumin in turmeric cream has been shown to combat periodontal bacteria, reducing inflammation and swelling around the affected area. Additionally, turmeric's ability to stimulate collagen production helps to strengthen gums and reduce pocket depths, promoting a healthy environment for gum tissue regeneration. As a result, turmeric creams can provide effective relief from gum infection symptoms, including pain, redness, and bleeding.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Turmeric's antimicrobial compounds, such as curcuminoids, help fight off bacteria and other microorganisms that cause gum infections by inhibiting their growth and preventing them from multiplying.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in turmeric cream, including curcumin, help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, which can alleviate pain and discomfort associated with gum infections.

Antioxidant properties: Turmeric's antioxidants, such as vitamin C and E, help protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to gum disease and infection.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Containing high levels of curcuminoids, turmeric rhyzomes provide potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties to combat gum infections.
Roots: Turmeric roots have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat oral health issues, including gum infections, due to their antiseptic and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality turmeric roots and grind them into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 20 grams of turmeric powder with 100 ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture at a low temperature of around 60°c to 70°c for 30 minutes to allow infusion and decolorization.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Allow the liquid to cool down to room temperature before filling and sealing the containers for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with gum infection because of its unique properties. The cream's main active ingredient, salvianolic acid, has potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory abilities that effectively combat periodontal pathogens. As a result, it reduces inflammation, kills bacteria, and promotes healing in the affected area. Additionally, sage creams stimulate blood flow to the gums, promoting healthy tissue regeneration and reducing pocket depth, thereby alleviating symptoms of gum infection. remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Herbal sage creams contain antimicrobial compounds like thymol, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections, such as Streptococcus mutans and Staphylococcus aureus.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage contains anti-inflammatory compounds like rosmarinic acid, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation associated with gum infections, promoting a faster recovery.

Antioxidant properties: Herbal sage creams contain antioxidants like carnosic acid, which help protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of tissue damage and promoting healthy gum tissue.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them effective in reducing gum inflammation and preventing bacterial growth.
Rhyzomes: The underground stems of sage plants, also known as rhyzomes, contain bioactive compounds that help combat gum infections by inhibiting the growth of pathogens.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves and chop them finely into small pieces.
Mix the chopped leaves with sweet almond oil in a ratio of 1:3 and let it steep for 24 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to extract the herbal essence.
Add beeswax and vitamin e oil to the extracted essence in a ratio of 2:1 and heat gently until melted.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum creams helps with gum infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which target the underlying causes of the infection. The cream's active ingredients, such as eucalyptus and tea tree oil, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause gum disease. Additionally, Tasmanian blue gum has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain in the affected area, allowing for faster healing and a reduced risk of complications.

Wound-healing properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum has been traditionally used to accelerate wound healing by improving blood flow, reducing scarring, and promoting tissue repair, making it an effective treatment for gum wounds and abscesses.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Tasmanian blue gum plant contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in treating gum infections.

remedy_recipe: Sterilize glass jars and utensils by submerging them in boiling water for 20 minutes.
Combine 30 grams of beeswax pellets, 50 grams of coconut oil, and 10 grams of eucalyptus globulus essential oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler until the wax is fully melted and reaches 180°f in 15 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool slightly before whipping with a hand mixer for 5 minutes.
Pour the whipped cream into sterilized glass jars, seal tightly, and store in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with gum infection because of its antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause inflammation in the gums. remedy_recipe: Harvest dried lavender flowers by collecting 2 tablespoons of dried lavandula angustifolia in a clean glass jar.
Combine the dried lavender flowers with 1/4 cup of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a small saucepan over low heat.
Heat the mixture for 10-15 minutes or until the beeswax has fully melted stirring occasionally to prevent scorching.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it cool for 5-7 minutes before pouring the cream into sterilized glass jars.
Store the cream in the refrigerator for up to 6 months allowing the ingredients to mature together for optimal potency.

remedy_recipe: Harvest syzygium aromaticum bark in late spring or early summer when it contains maximum amount of active compounds.

Grind 30 grams of dried syzygium aromaticum bark into fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground powder with 100 grams of coconut oil and 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for 1 hour.
Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature before whipping with an electric mixer until smooth and creamy texture achieved.

remedy_recipe: Weigh out one hundred grams of petroleum jelly and melt it slowly in a double boiler.
Add fifteen milliliters of distilled water to the melted petroleum jelly and stir until fully incorporated.
Combine thirty grams of dried serenoa repens root powder with twenty-five milliliters of glycerin and mix thoroughly.
Gradually add the serenoa repens mixture to the melted petroleum jelly and water mixture while stirring constantly.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for at least two hours before transferring it to air-tight containers.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and grind them into a fine powder.
Mix 100 grams of the powdered root with 200 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Add 150 grams of coconut oil and heat the mixture for 30 minutes or until it reaches 60°c.
Remove from heat and stir in 50 grams of vitamin e oil and 20 grams of tea tree essential oil.
Pour the mixture into sterilized jars and let it cool and solidify at room temperature.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves, measuring out exactly 2 ounces for the recipe.
Steam distill the peppermint leaves in a still or using a steam distillation kit for 2 hours.
Combine the essential oil extracted from the steaming process with 8 tablespoons of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax.
Melt the mixture of coconut oil, beeswax, and peppermint essential oil in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes.
Pour the melted cream base into glass jars, allowing it to cool and solidify completely before use.

Mix the powdered rosemary leaves with 200ml of distilled water and 20g of beeswax to create a smooth paste.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax melts and forms a creamy consistency.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and roots in the morning after dew has evaporated ensuring optimal potency.
Dry the harvested foeniculum vulgare material using a food dehydrator at 35 degrees celsius for 24 hours to preserve quality.
Combine one part dried foeniculum vulgare leaves with two parts coconut oil and infuse in a warm dark place for 14 days allowing for optimal extraction.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding solids and filtering resulting liquid through a coffee filter for clarity.
Mix one part of the filtered foeniculum vulgare cream with one part beeswax and melt at 65 degrees celsius in a double boiler whipping until smooth and consistent texture is achieved.

Mix the powdered anethum graveolens with 20% petroleum jelly in a ratio of 1:3 by weight.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for gum infection are natural topical treatments that utilize the medicinal properties of herbs to alleviate symptoms and promote healing. These creams contain ingredients such as tea tree oil, aloe vera, and chamomile, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help reduce swelling, ease pain, and combat bacterial growth. For example, creams containing eucalyptus and peppermint can help soothe inflamed gums, while those with myrrh and cloves can aid in the elimination of bacteria. By using herbal creams, individuals can enjoy improved oral health, reduced discomfort, and increased confidence in their smile.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat gum infection with herbal creams is to use natural ingredients that have antimicrobial properties. Apply a cream containing tea tree oil, which has antibacterial effects, directly to the affected area 2-3 times a day. Additionally, use a cream enriched with chamomile, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, to reduce swelling and discomfort. This holistic approach can help alleviate symptoms and promote healing of the gum infection.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gum infection that are treated with herbal creams are skin conditions such as acne, eczema, and psoriasis. Herbal creams containing ingredients like tea tree oil, aloe vera, and chamomile have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help soothe and heal irritated skin. They also combat fungal and bacterial infections, promoting healthy skin and reducing inflammation.
